title: Terminal Value: The terminal value in a DCF model represents the expected...
summary: Evaluate the role of terminal value in capturing cash flows beyond the explicit forecast period.
prompt: The terminal value in a DCF model represents the expected cash flows beyond the forecast period.
solution: Terminal value is critical in a DCF analysis as it captures the value of a firm beyond the explicit forecast years. It is calculated based on the expected stable growth of cash flows into perpetuity.
